slides.title: Shapes Vocabulary
slides.content:
Let's learn the names of shapes we see in art:

🔺 triangle — a shape with 3 sides
🔷 diamond — a shape like a square turned on its side
🥚 oval — a shape like a stretched circle (like an egg)
🌀 spiral — a line that goes round and round, getting bigger
slides.tip: Triangle = tri (three) + angle. A triangle always has THREE sides!

slides.title: Art and Nature Words
slides.content:
More vocabulary about shapes in art and nature:

🔁 pattern — a design that repeats again and again
📏 straight — not curved, going in one direction
📸 photograph — a picture taken with a camera
🌿 nature — the world around us (trees, flowers, animals)
slides.tip: Look for patterns in nature! Flowers, leaves, and shells all have beautiful patterns.

slides.title: Art Project Words
slides.content:
Words for making art:

📐 size — how big or small something is
✏️ drawing — a picture made with a pencil or pen
🎠 mobile — a hanging decoration that moves in the air
🦢 origami — the art of folding paper into shapes

Example: We made a mobile with origami birds of different sizes.

slides.title: Shapes Everywhere
slides.html:
Miss Taylor asked her class to find shapes in nature.

Emma took a photograph of a spider's web. It had a beautiful spiral pattern! Jack found triangles in the mountains and ovals in the leaves.

Back in class, they made drawings of what they found. Lily drew a flower with diamond shapes for petals. She used straight lines for the stem.

Then they made a mobile with all their shapes in different sizes. It was beautiful!

slides.title: Are there any...?
slides.content:
We use Are there any...? to ask about things in a place:

❓ Are there any triangles in this picture?
✅ Yes, there are.
❌ No, there aren't.

We use There isn't any... and There aren't any... to say there is NONE:

🔹 There isn't any paper. (singular/uncountable)
🔹 There aren't any circles. (plural)
slides.tip: ANY is used in questions and negative sentences. Use SOME in positive sentences!

slides.title: Questions with Any
slides.problem:
Complete the sentences with Are there any, There isn't any or There aren't any:

1. _____ spirals in the drawing?
2. _____ diamonds in this pattern.
3. _____ glue on the table.
4. _____ ovals in the photograph?
steps: 1. A question about plural shapes --> Are there any
2. Negative sentence, plural (diamonds) --> There aren't any
3. Negative sentence, singular/uncountable (glue) --> There isn't any
4. A question about plural shapes --> Are there any
slides.answer: 1. Are there any  2. There aren't any  3. There isn't any  4. Are there any

slides.title: Homophones
slides.content:
Homophones are words that sound the same but have different meanings and different spellings:

👀 see / sea — to look / the ocean
🌺 flower / flour — a plant / white powder for baking
✍️ write / right — to put words on paper / correct
👂 hear / here — to listen / in this place
slides.tip: Homo = same, phone = sound. Homophones sound the same but mean different things!

slides.title: Choose the Right Homophone
slides.problem:
Choose the correct word:

1. I can (see / sea) a triangle in the picture.
2. Can you (hear / here) the music?
3. Please (write / right) your name.
4. We need (flower / flour) to make a cake.

slides.title: The Art Class
slides.html:
“Are there any scissors?” asked Tom. “Yes, here they are,” said Miss Taylor.

Today they are making origami shapes. Tom makes a diamond. “Can you see the pattern?” he asks.

“There aren't any triangles in my drawing,” says Emma. “I only have spirals and ovals.”

“That's right!” says Miss Taylor. “Write the name of each shape under your drawing.”

slides.title: Find the Shapes
slides.problem:
Look at this description and answer the questions:

The picture has 3 triangles, 2 ovals, and 1 spiral. There are no diamonds or straight lines.

1. Are there any triangles?
2. Are there any diamonds?
3. Are there any spirals?
steps: 1. The picture has 3 triangles --> Yes, there are.
2. There are no diamonds --> No, there aren't.
3. The picture has 1 spiral --> Yes, there is.
slides.answer: 1. Yes, there are.  2. No, there aren't.  3. Yes, there is.

slides.title: Let's Review!
points: 🎨 Art words: pattern, straight, photograph, nature, size, drawing, mobile, origami
❓ Are there any...? --> Yes, there are. / No, there aren't.
🚫 There isn't any... (singular) / There aren't any... (plural)
🔊 Homophones: words that sound the same but have different meanings (see/sea, write/right, hear/here, flower/flour)
